Automates locking of inactive closed issues, pull requests, and discussions after 14 days.

# Purpose
The configuration file defines a scheduled task that automatically locks closed issues, pull requests, and discussions in a repository after 14 days of inactivity. The task runs daily at midnight, as specified by the cron schedule. It uses the `dessant/lock-threads` GitHub Action to perform the locking operation. The file grants write permissions to issues, pull requests, and discussions, ensuring that the action can modify these elements. The concurrency setting groups the task under the name `lock`, which helps manage the execution of the job to prevent overlapping runs.
